summary: Martin Heidegger's philosophical project centers on ontology, initially exploring the meaning of being through the phenomenological analysis of human existence (Dasein). Later, he shifted toward language, poetry, and historical exegesis to pursue non-metaphysical thinking. He critiqued Western metaphysics as nihilistic for obliterating the question of being, ultimately concluding that being inherently withdraws as it discloses itself.

principles.statement: The question of being must be approached through the phenomenological analysis of human existence.
principles.rationale: To access being (Sein), one must analyze human existence (Dasein) with respect to its temporal and historical character.
principles.source_quote: he attempted to access being (Sein) by means of phenomenological analysis of human existence (Dasein) in respect to its temporal and historical character.

principles.statement: Being inherently withdraws even as it reveals itself in history.
principles.rationale: Being has multiple historical determinations, but it never gives itself fully. The truth of being cannot be reduced to any single historical concept because of this essential withdrawal.
principles.source_quote: As it discloses itself in beings, being withdraws.

principles.statement: Philosophy is incapable of directly changing the current state of the world.
principles.rationale: The scope and greatness of philosophical thought are too vast to be reduced to direct, immediate worldly interventions.
principles.source_quote: Philosophy will not be able to bring about a direct change of the present state of the world . . . The greatness of what is to be thought is too great.

frameworks.name: Phenomenological Analysis of Dasein
frameworks.when_to_apply: When attempting to access and understand the fundamental meaning of being (Sein).
steps: Identify human existence (Dasein) as the primary subject of analysis.
Analyze Dasein in respect to its temporal character.
Analyze Dasein in respect to its historical character.
frameworks.source_quote: he attempted to access being (Sein) by means of phenomenological analysis of human existence (Dasein) in respect to its temporal and historical character.

mental_models.name: Disclosing Self-Concealing (The Withdrawal of Being)
mental_models.description: The concept that being reveals itself through specific historical determinations, but simultaneously hides its ultimate essence in the very act of revealing.
mental_models.example: Historical concepts like idea, substantia, actualitas, objectivity, or the will to power are partial disclosures of being, but being itself withdraws from being fully captured by any of them.

heuristics: Turn to language, poetry, and historical exegesis to unfold the question of being, rather than relying on traditional metaphysics.

signature_quotes.text: Philosophy will not be able to bring about a direct change of the present state of the world . . . The greatness of what is to be thought is too great.
signature_quotes.context: From his famous interview with Der Spiegel, expressing disillusionment with the idea that philosophy can directly alter the world's state.

signature_quotes.text: As it discloses itself in beings, being withdraws.
signature_quotes.context: Describing the essential withdrawal of being across its multiple historical determinations.

anti_patterns.description: Relying on the tradition of Western metaphysics to understand being.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It is nihilistic and obliterates the question of being as such by reducing it to particular historical determinations.

anti_patterns.description: Embracing positivism and technological world domination.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It aligns with the metaphysical tradition that obscures the true question of being and attempts to set up fabricated ends for human beings.
